title: mfa — compliance evidence
description: MFA is one of the most compliance-material layers of the platform. Every applicable rule from NIST 800-63B (AAL2 authenticator strength), FIDO Alliance Level 2 (WebAuthn attestation verification), PCI-DSS 4.0 (MFA for administrative access), GDPR (data minimisation on device metadata), and SOC 2 Type 2 (access controls + monitoring) applies here. Every enforcement is codified as CODE + AUDIT — no policy documents to interpret at runtime.

controls.evidence: Password (memorized secret, handled by identity/identity) + TOTP (§ 5.1.4 OTP device) OR WebAuthn (§ 5.1.7 single-factor cryptographic device / § 5.1.8 multi-factor cryptographic software) satisfies AAL2. `mfa.required_for_roles` (default: owner + admin + billing_manager + super_admin) enforces AAL2 for privileged principals via `mfa.require_challenge_complete` middleware.

TOTP secret is 160 bits (RFC 6238 recommended, above NIST's 80-bit minimum). Preview-then-confirm enrolment (§ 5.1.4.2 verifier lifecycle: proof-of-possession before persistence) — the plaintext never persists before the user demonstrates they scanned it. Constant-time verifier (§ 5.1.4.3 timing-attack resistance).

WebAuthn platform authenticator (Face ID, Touch ID, Windows Hello) satisfies § 5.1.7. Attestation verification runs at registration; sign-count monotonic invariant enforced at every assertion (§ 5.1.7.2 verifier requirements).

WebAuthn with `user_verification: required` and a resident-key-supporting authenticator satisfies § 5.1.8 as a single-step MFA. Our default (`user_verification: preferred`) satisfies § 5.1.7 (single-factor cryptographic device); tenants can tighten to § 5.1.8 via settings.

TOTP: the (identity_id, challenge_id, code) tuple can be consumed once (challenge row is marked consumed atomically). WebAuthn: sign-count monotonic invariant + credential.disabled_at flag guard against cloned-authenticator replay. Every replay attempt is rejected with `SIGN_COUNT_REPLAY_DETECTED` (422) + records a critical audit entry.

Every WebAuthn registration verifies the attestation statement against the format-specific verifier (packed / fido-u2f / android-key / apple / tpm — implemented by `web-auth/webauthn-lib`). Config `mfa.webauthn.attestation_conveyance` defaults to `none` (privacy-preserving); tenants with FIDO MDS compliance mandates set to `direct` and reject authenticators absent from the FIDO Alliance Metadata Service.
The assertion verifier checks the UV flag matches the requested `user_verification` policy. Mismatch → refusal + `WEBAUTHN_UV_POLICY_VIOLATION` (422).
The RP ID (`config('mfa.webauthn.rp_id')`) is bound at registration + verified at assertion. Prevents cross-origin attestations from being replayed against Stackra.

`mfa.required_for_roles` includes billing_manager + owner + admin + super_admin by default. Every tenant-audience action on the billing surface (subscription changes, refunds, chargebacks) traverses `mfa.require_challenge_complete` before reaching the controller. Compliance auditor can prove enforcement via the metrics stream: `stackra.mfa.step_up.enforced` is non-zero on every billing surface.

WebauthnCredential carries only the cryptographic material + user-provided device_name + technical metadata (transports, AAGUID, sign_count). No IP, no user-agent, no browser fingerprint — those live in identity/auth's login trail, not here. Even the AAGUID (which identifies the authenticator model) is classified internal-only and never exposed to tenant callers.

WebauthnCredential.identity_id FK is CASCADE on delete — an Identity hard-purge in identity/identity's PurgeSoftDeletedIdentitiesJob drops every credential in the same transaction. MFA disable also purges the recovery-codes JSONB atomically. No stale MFA material survives an erasure.

TOTP secret plaintext never persists (in-memory during enrolment preview only). WebAuthn public key is stored — public by design of WebAuthn — but the corresponding private key never leaves the authenticator. Recovery codes are bcrypt-hashed at rest. sign_count is monotonic-checked to defeat cloned-authenticator replay.

Constant-time comparisons (`hash_equals()`) on every code / recovery-code / assertion verify. Rate-limited enrolment previews (`mfa.challenge.cooldown_minutes_after_max`). Progressive challenge invalidation after `max_attempts`. Suspicious-activity detection with fleet-wide notification dispatch.

Two guards enforced (sanctum, platform_admin) with role scoping. All privileged MFA operations (force-disable, regenerate-recovery-codes) are audit-logged with the operator's Identity + reason. Recovery codes are single-use by construction.
Rate limiting + progressive-attempt caps + suspicious-activity detection. All threats emit metrics + alerts. sign_count replay detection is a distinct alert channel.

Health probes (mfa-schema-current, mfa-totp-library-loaded, mfa-webauthn-library-loaded, mfa-encryptor-reachable, mfa-challenge-ledger-reachable, mfa-suspicious-activity-scheduler-alive, mfa-retention-current) + comprehensive OpenTelemetry metrics feed the operational monitoring pipeline.

controls.criterion: A.5.16 identity management + A.5.17 authentication information
controls.evidence: Every state transition (enrolment, challenge, credential registration / disable / delete, recovery-code regen) is captured as an event + written to the activity_log via HasActivityLog composition. Enables the auditor to reconstruct any user's MFA history in a compliance interview.
Cryptographic authenticator strength + secret encryption at rest (delegated to identity/identity's MfaSecretEncryptor) + timing-safe verifier + attempt limits.

audit_evidence_endpoints.description: Endpoints an auditor can hit (with appropriate platform-admin credentials) to gather compliance evidence WITHOUT touching production data.

endpoints.purpose: Prove per-user MFA posture — which factors enrolled, credential count.

Enumerate the last N days of MFA-lifecycle events (enrolments, challenges, credential lifecycles, disables) at the tenant or fleet level. Redacted — no plaintext, no bytea, only aggregated counts + timestamps.
Enumerate Identities that hold role=admin but lack MFA (past the grace period). Compliance-violation report.
